entry.title: #SITRIO2025 Onde o caldo entorna - Como superar os maiores desafios na implementação do DRC NFE
entry.content:
Se você trabalha com SAP, localização Brasil ou NF-e, já deve ter ouvido falar sobre a migração do GRC para o DRC NFE. E se ainda não enfrentou essa transição, prepare-se: ela não é trivial. Por isso, o título da palestra de Renan Correa no SAP Inside Track Rio 2025 não poderia ser mais apropriado: "Onde o caldo entorna - Como superar os maiores desafios na implementação do DRC NFE".

Sobre o palestrante

Renan Correa é consultor SAP desde 2009, com experiência aprofundada em localização Brasil, NF-e e eInvoice para toda a América Latina. Ele é membro ativo da comunidade SAP, autor de blogs, artigos e livros técnicos, e também atua como instrutor de treinamentos nas áreas de SD/NFe, DRC e TDF. Além disso, é conhecido pela sua abordagem direta e didática sobre temas complexos.

O que esperar da palestra

Durante sua apresentação no evento, Renan vai guiar o público pelos principais percalços e soluções na implementação do DRC NFE:

  • Testes insuficientes e ambientes mal configurados: uma das principais fontes de falhas está na migração incorreta de ambientes de testes para produção, gerando “dumps” e inconsistências.

  • PDFs e XMLs exigem customização: funcionalidades antes comuns como anexos de PDF ou carta de correção precisam ser redesenvolvidas no novo cenário.

  • Diferenças sutis, mas impactantes, entre GRC e DRC: o mapeamento de XMLs pode parecer igual, mas detalhes como caracteres especiais e campos obrigatórios geram rejeições.

  • A dor de cabeça chamada NFS-e: a nota fiscal de serviço é um dos pontos mais críticos, exigindo configurações e desenvolvimentos específicos por município.

  • Automatização de entrada é complexa: exige criação de diversas RFCs, revisão de BAdIs e ajustes em funções herdadas do GRC.

  • Processos Z: o segredo da flexibilidade: essenciais para adaptar o cockpit do DRC, os processos customizados demandam tempo e planejamento.

  • Retreinamento de usuários é essencial: mesmo com lógica semelhante, as telas são diferentes, e subestimar essa mudança compromete o sucesso do projeto.

Renan traz à tona uma visão realista e fundamentada nos projetos que participou, alertando sobre estimativas fantasiosas, resistências do cliente e a necessidade de um planejamento pragmático.

Se você é consultor, gestor de projeto ou trabalha na área fiscal de empresas que utilizam SAP, essa é uma palestra imperdível. Além de aprender como evitar que "o caldo entorne", você vai sair mais preparado para garantir uma implementação de sucesso.

Assista agora
Se você quer estar preparado para a realidade do TaaS e entender os impactos do fim do TAXBRA no seu dia a dia profissional, não perca esse vídeo. Acesse a palestra completa na gravação oficial do SAP Inside Track Rio 2025 e fique por dentro de tudo.

SAP Inside Track Rio de Janeiro 2025

 

Se você deseja ser notificado sobre os próximos eventos SAP Inside Track no Brasil, entre na nossa lista de interesse: Cadastre-se aqui para ser notificado

entry.title: Actions in Behavior Definition For CDS Custom Entity
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

The scenario is;  I have created a RAP application that will display data from Web Service. CDS Custom entity was created and the query implementation class called Web service.

The next step is to save the data from my Fiori Report List into my custom Z* table and update the "Status" field on the Fiori List.

Why does the Read Entity result fail? 

What should be the way?

Kind regards, aatan.

highlighted items should be automatically cleared under the outgoing clearing account.

Melle

entry.title: Re: BAPI_MATERIAL_SAVEREPLICA | Z-fields cannot be updated
entry.content:
I am back to the topic; unfortunately there are many topics (sorted by priority).

So far the update of the Z-fields does not work. We are currently working on the basis of the current release status (latest S/4HANA release).

We are currently updating the Z fields directly via an update.

If you have any ideas, I would be very happy to hear them.

Thank you very much for your support.

Kind regards
Kurt

entry.title: Re: Quota issue
entry.content:
Hi Pravat_Rath,
I had the same issue deploying CAL - S4H 2023 SP2 on GCP, but it was resolved when I followed these steps:
1. I deleted the appliance with the CAL error (Status Terminated).
2. I added more resources in the Google Cloud console: IAM & Admin / Quotas & System Limits
- Service: Compute Engine API: Persistent Disk SSD (GB) for the deployment region (example: region: europe-west1)
- Service: Compute Engine API: CPUs (all regions)
3. I created a new appliance and the connection was successful.
If you're still having trouble connecting, you could try this. I've provided two images for reference.
Regards,
Dante

entry.title: Re: Create purchase order with different invoice recipient
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

what does that mean "not yet use the SAP S/4 HANA Cloud in this way"? Does that mean you only need to set it up? 

Nevertheless, the advantages of using purchase orders between internal companies are:

- only the creation of an purchase order in the ordering plant automatically creates the purchase order call off in the delivering plant. Here it is similar to a standard customer order.

- you can generate the purchasing price automatically from the valuation price in the material master of the delivering plant

- overview about the delivered materials if they are in transit

- after creating the invoice for the purchase order in the receiving plant, you can generate the invoice receipt via IDOC automatically

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: Re: Find source code of fiori applications in Business Ap...
entry.content: then you are doomed

entry.title: Re: Supplier Evaluation Query
entry.content:
Dear former_member114268,

1. The main settings of the evaluation criteria takes place within the system outside the standard customizing in CBC. So you need to define your criteria used and how much with the Supplier Evaluation Weighting and Scoring.

 

If you use the purchasing category function, you are able to set up different supplier evaluation by purchasing category (for example according to material groups or branch).

 

 

The same for the supplier questionnaires: Here you also set up the most within the system. Needed apps are at least:

a) Create Question Library for Supplier Evaluation

b) Manage Evaluation Questionnaires

c) Manage Evaluation Templates (for the questionnaires)  

d) Translate Evaluation Templates

e)Translate Questions

2. Yes, the Questionnaires can be sent by Mail automatically.

3. I didn’t see such function until today but you could provide your supplier via Integration Suite and custom app the possibility to enter the information directly in the system. Would be the most modern way and avoids managing an interface for the transferring data between systems.

Best Regards

Dennis

entry.title: How to enable single selection in a Fiori Element list report
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I'm working with SAP RAP OData V4, now when using OData V2 the list report is using radio button for selection so i can select only one record but when using V4 it uses a checkbox so i can select multiple records as shown below.

My question is, is it possible to change the checkbox to a radio button or add certain annotation so that i can only select one record.
Below is my metadata extension. 

@Metadata.layer: #PARTNER
@UI:{
   headerInfo:{ typeName: 'Congé',
                typeNamePlural: 'Liste des congés',
                title: { type: #STANDARD, label: 'Congé', value: 'Id' }},
                presentationVariant: [{ sortOrder: [{ by: 'Status', direction: #DESC }] }]
   }

annotate entity ZC_CONGE_RH
    with 
{


 @ui.facet: [{ id : 'Conge',
               purpose: #STANDARD,
               type: #IDENTIFICATION_REFERENCE,
               label: 'Detail de congé',
               position : 10
            }]   
    
 
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 10, label: 'Id congé' }], identification: [{ position: 10, label: 'Id congé' }] }        
    Id;
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 20, label: 'Type congé' }], identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Type congé' }] }
    @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity: { name:'ZI_CONGE_DOM', element: 'Value' },
    distinctValues: true }]
    Type;
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 30, label: 'Date debut congé' }], identification: [{ position: 30, label: 'Date debut congé' }] }
    Dob;
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 40, label: 'Date fin congé' }], identification: [{ position: 40, label: 'Date fin congé' }] }
    Doe;
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 50, label: 'Status' }, 
                      { type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'setValidation', label: 'Validate'}, 
                      { type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'setRejection', label: 'Reject'}], 
           
           identification: [{ position: 50, label: 'Status' }, 
                            { type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'setValidation', label: 'Validate'}, 
                            { type: #FOR_ACTION, dataAction: 'setRejection', label: 'Reject'}] 
         }
    Status;
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 60, label: 'Demandé par' }], 
           identification: [{ position: 60, label: 'Demandé par' }] 
         }
    Createdby;
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 65, label: 'Créé à' }], 
           identification: [{ position: 65, label: 'Créé à' }] 
         }
    CreatedAt;
    @ui: { lineItem: [{ position: 70, label: 'Date Validation / Rejection' }], identification: [{ position: 70, label: 'Date de Validation / Rejection' }] }
    ValidationDate;   
    
     
    
}

And this is my data definition Interface

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface conge'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define root view entity ZI_CONGE_RH
  as select from zpfe_conge_rh
  association to ZI_CONGE_DOM as _type on $projection.Type = _type.Value
{
  key id                as Id,
      type              as Type,
      dob               as Dob,
      doe               as Doe,
      status            as Status,
      createdby         as Createdby,
      createdat         as CreatedAt,
      validation_date   as ValidationDate,
      joursconge        as JoursConge,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.lastChangedAt: true
      lastchangedat     as Lastchangedat,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
      locallastchange   as Locallastchange,
      _type,
      _type.Description as TypeDesc
}

And the consumption view

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Consumption view conge'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
    serviceQuality: #X,
    sizeCategory: #S,
    dataClass: #MIXED
}
define root view entity ZC_CONGE_RH as projection on ZI_CONGE_RH
{
    @EndUserText.label: 'Id congé'
    key Id,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Type de congé'
    Type,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Date debut congé'
    Dob,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Date fin congé'
    Doe,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Status'
    Status,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Créé par'
    Createdby,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Créé à'
    CreatedAt,
    @EndUserText.label: 'Date de validation'
    ValidationDate,
    @ui.hidden: true
    JoursConge,
    @ui.hidden: true
    Lastchangedat,
    @ui.hidden: true
    Locallastchange
}

Was wondering if you would be able to share how we would be able to resolve the issue of the save data entity button being grayed out. I tried to fill out the form in several different ways throughout the past few weeks and nothing has worked. Thank you very much in advance!

entry.title: Re: LangChain vs LlamaIndex: Enhancing LLM Applications on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Feature / Capability LangChain LlamaIndex

Primary FocusMulti-step workflows, agent orchestration, and tool chainingData ingestion, indexing, and semantic retrieval for LLMs
Typical Use CasesChatbots, assistants, tool agents, and dynamic workflowsKnowledge base Q&A, smart document search, semantic querying
Data HandlingWorks best when paired with external data tools (like LlamaIndex)Specializes in document parsing, embeddings, and search
LLM Skill Usage- Natural Language Understanding (NLU)
- Text generation
- Agent planning & memory		- Semantic search
- Document QA
- Text chunking/indexing
Supported LanguagesPython only (official support, rich ecosystem)
Experimental TypeScript support via LangChain.js		Python only (no official Java or other SDKs yet)
SAP BTP IntegrationIdeal for CAP-based apps using Kyma, BTP services via REST/GraphQL APIsActs as a backend indexing layer, exposed via Python microservices
Ease of UseMore modular and customizable – steep initial learning curveSimpler for retrieval-based use cases – quick to prototype
Common PairingWorks best with LlamaIndex for RAG use casesOften used underneath LangChain to provide a retrieval layer

 

🛠️ Practical Examples in SAP BTP

1. LangChain in Action

Scenario: Developing a customer support chatbot that can handle multi-turn conversations and perform actions like checking order status.​

Implementation:

  • Utilize LangChain to create a conversational agent that maintains context across interactions.

  • Integrate with SAP's APIs to fetch real-time order data.

  • Employ memory modules to remember user preferences or previous interactions.​

2. LlamaIndex in Action

Scenario: Creating a knowledge base search tool for internal SAP documentation.​

Implementation:

  • Use LlamaIndex to ingest and index vast amounts of SAP documentation.

  • Enable semantic search capabilities to allow users to query the knowledge base in natural language.

  • Ensure efficient retrieval of relevant documents or sections based on user queries.

🔗 Synergizing LangChain and LlamaIndex on SAP BTP

Combining the strengths of both frameworks can lead to powerful applications.​

Use Case: An intelligent assistant that provides SAP users with precise answers from internal documents and performs tasks based on user requests.​

Integration Strategy:

  1. Data Handling: LlamaIndex ingests and indexes SAP's internal documents, creating a robust retrieval system.

  2. User Interaction: LangChain manages the conversational flow, interprets user intents, and maintains context.

  3. Combined Workflow:

    • The user poses a question.

    • LangChain processes the query and uses LlamaIndex to retrieve relevant information.

    • LangChain presents the answer and, if required, performs additional actions like creating a support ticket.

SAP Learning Journey: Integrating LLMs into Business Applications

entry.title: How do I retrieve Associated Employee in custom script in SAP Cloud studio ?
entry.content:
I have created a Business object and one of the elements is an association to Employee  : -

[Label("EmployeeID")] element EmployeeID : EmployeeID;
association ToEmployee to Employee;

So I am not able to retrieve the employee in my custom script using the below code : -

var retrieveEmployee=BusinessPartner.Retrieve(this.ToEmployee.UUID);

The error is UUID does not exist.

Help.

entry.title: Re: MSSQL Setup on MAC using bacpac dump from Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Thank you For the article!

While following the guide, I realized there’s an important real-life step that we should not miss:

The active typesystem version in the database is not always the DEFAULT.
In many projects, the DEFAULT version can be marked as "retired", and a newer typesystem version is active.

To fix it, add this to your local.properties :

 

db.type.system.name=<the active version name>

You can find the active version by:

Checking in 𝗛𝗔𝗖 of the source environment

OR

Running the following SQL:

 

𝗦𝗘𝗟𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗻𝗮𝗺𝗲 𝗙𝗥𝗢𝗠 𝗖𝗖𝗩𝟮_𝗧𝗬𝗣𝗘𝗦𝗬𝗦𝗧𝗘𝗠_𝗠𝗜𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗜𝗢𝗡𝗦 𝗪𝗛𝗘𝗥𝗘 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗲 = '𝗰𝘂𝗿𝗿𝗲𝗻𝘁'

entry.title: Re: Vehicle Data Migration
entry.content: What actions need to be performed to migrate vehicles relevant for warranty? We have created the vehicle with VIN details. I am unable to assign a dealer or end customer or status like stolen.

entry.title: Options from SAP to archive HCM employee historical records
entry.content:
Hi,

Can anyone suggest what solutions/services SAP has given to store HCM employee historical data from non-sap legacy system? or in other words if client is moving from non-SAP system to SAP SuccessFactors, then where can employee history from non-SAP legacy system can be archived in SAP ecosystem/landscape?

I know about third party options like OpenText, Snowflake, google cloud etc. but I am wondering what storage options has SAP has given for SAP customers? Also, has anyone seen SAP HANA Cloud database/Postgre (Data services of SAP BTP) used to archive historical records?

Also, can SAP ILM be used for this purpose? As per my understanding SAP ILM is mostly suited for existing SAP customers i.e., who want to archive data from their ECC (Sap legacy system). SAP ILM is not a flexible option if we want to archive data from a non-SAP system.

Regards,

entry.title: Re: EDIT HIRE RECORD BY IMPORT
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for the information in detail.

I understand you would like to edit hire record via incremental load import. But It is not possible to do that as error message "There is already a hire record for user tsmith. You cannot create another hire record if one already exists.," will pop up. I hope this explanation helps.

 

Best regards,

Hannah

entry.title: Streamlining Alert Management in SAP HANA Cloud: Leveraging REST APIs for Bulk Alert Configuration
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud has introduced powerful capabilities for alert configuration management, including bulk alert configuration through REST APIs. This enhancement provides administrators with granular control over system monitoring while simplifying the management of alerts across multiple instances. The ability to programmatically configure alerts represents a significant advancement in operational efficiency for SAP HANA Cloud environments.

Understanding Alerts in SAP HANA Cloud

Alerts in SAP HANA Cloud serve as an essential early warning system, notifying administrators of potential issues before they become critical problems. The system generates alerts based on specific metrics like CPU utilization, disk usage, or user transactions, with predefined thresholds determining when an alert is triggered.

Alerts are categorized by severity levels – ranging from Information (lowest) to Error (highest) – enabling administrators to prioritize their responses effectively. When a metric measurement exceeds its designated threshold, an alert is triggered. Once the metric returns below the threshold, the alert automatically resolves itself.

Within SAP HANA Cloud Central, administrators can view both active and resolved alerts across all instances within a subaccount. The platform provides filtering options based on severity level, instance, and time range, allowing for precise alert monitoring and management.

Alert Configuration APIs

The introduction of REST APIs for alert configuration represents a significant enhancement to SAP HANA Cloud's administration capabilities. These APIs enable programmatic control over alert rules, thresholds, and statuses, facilitating more efficient and consistent management practices.

Key API Endpoint for Alert Configuration

Of particular importance is the PATCH endpoint for updating alert rules:

 

PATCH /alerts/v1/serviceInstances/{serviceInstanceID}/rules/

 

This API allows administrators to update the status and thresholds of specific alert rules for a designated service instance. The API can be used to update both cloud-native rules and ESS (embedded statistics server) rules, providing comprehensive coverage for your alert management needs.

To utilize this API, you need:

  • A subaccount level access token for authentication
  • The service instance ID specified in the path parameter
  • A properly formatted request body that specifies:
    • The alert rule name
    • Severity threshold values (such as INFO, WARNING, ERROR)
    • The enabled status (true/false)

Example API Request

Here's an example of how to update the threshold values for a disk usage alert:

 

PATCH {{gateway_url}}/alerts/v1/serviceInstances/{{instanceID}}/rules
Authorization: Bearer {{bearer}}

{
  "data": [
    {
      "alertRule": "HDBDiskUsage",
      "severities": {
        "NOTICE": { "threshold": "91" },
        "WARNING": { "threshold": "96" },
        "ERROR": { "threshold": "99" }
      },
      "enabled": true
    }
  ]
}

 

After making changes, you can verify the updated threshold values with:

 

GET {{gateway_url}}/alerts/v1/serviceInstances/{{instanceID}}/rules/HDBDiskUsage
Authorization: Bearer {{bearer}}

Implementing Bulk Alert Configuration

The real power of the REST APIs becomes evident when configuring alerts across multiple service instances simultaneously. As of recent updates, SAP HANA Cloud now supports using an API to update the alert rules of multiple service instances in an SAP HANA Cloud Central subaccount.

Prerequisites for Bulk Configuration

To implement bulk alert configuration, you'll need:

  1. Service Plan Access: Ensure you have the admin-api-access service plan enabled under the SAP HANA Cloud service. Note that this service plan is not available for trial instances.
  2. Technical User Setup: Create a service instance with the appropriate parameters.
  3. Authentication: Generate and manage access tokens properly for API authentication.

Bulk Configuration Example

Here's how to update the threshold of a specific alert for multiple instances:

PATCH {{gateway_url}}/alerts/v1/rules
Authorization: Bearer {{bearer}}

{
  "data": [
    {
      "serviceInstanceID": "{{instanceID1}}",
      "alertRule": "HDBDiskUsage",
      "severities": {
        "NOTICE": { "threshold": "89" },
        "WARNING": { "threshold": "94" },
        "ERROR": { "threshold": "97" }
      },
      "enabled": true
    },
    {
      "serviceInstanceID": "{{instanceID2}}",
      "alertRule": "HDBDiskUsage",
      "severities": {
        "NOTICE": { "threshold": "85" },
        "WARNING": { "threshold": "92" },
        "ERROR": { "threshold": "98" }
      },
      "enabled": true
    }
  ]
}

This approach dramatically reduces the administrative overhead of maintaining consistent alert configurations across multiple instances.

Best Practices for Alert Configuration Management

When implementing alert configuration via APIs, consider these best practices:

  1. Version Control: Maintain your alert configuration scripts in a version control system to track changes and enable rollback if needed.
  2. Environment Segregation: Use different configuration sets for development, testing, and production environments.
  3. Documentation: Document your alert thresholds and the rationale behind them to facilitate knowledge transfer.
  4. Regular Reviews: Periodically review alert thresholds to ensure they remain appropriate as your system grows or usage patterns change.
  5. Monitoring Effectiveness: Track the signal-to-noise ratio of your alerts and adjust thresholds to minimize false positives while ensuring critical issues are caught.

SAP HANA Cloud Alerts and Metrics Using a REST API Tutorial

To make it convenient for you to consume alerts and metrics via the REST API, we recommend the SAP tutorial "Explore SAP HANA Cloud Alerts and Metrics". Find it here.

Conclusion

The introduction of REST APIs for bulk alert configuration in SAP HANA Cloud represents a significant advancement in operational efficiency. By enabling programmatic control over alert rules across multiple instances, SAP has provided administrators with the tools needed to implement consistent, scalable monitoring practices.

As cloud environments continue to grow in complexity, such automation capabilities become increasingly essential for maintaining system health and performance. By leveraging these APIs effectively, organizations can reduce administrative overhead while improving their ability to proactively address potential issues before they impact business operations.

entry.title: Inquiry Regarding Satellite Registration of New SAP ERP System in Solution Manager
entry.content:
We are currently in the process of replacing our SAP ERP system (same SID, different hostname). Our Solution Manager will remain unchanged and we need to register the new system as a satellite system in the existing Solution Manager.


After transferring the configuration information to SLD via RZ70, it appears that the technical system entry for our existing environment has been overwritten with the information from the new environment.


Could you please advise on the proper procedure to register both the existing and new environments as satellite systems?


For your information, our SLD is connected to Solution Manager via our central PI server.


Thank you for your time and assistance.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry Regarding Satellite Registration of New SAP E...
entry.content:
Hi *,

You are currently replacing your SAP ERP system (same SID, different hostname), and the old SAP ERP system will not be used anymore, right? If yes, you can check Note 1727294, which describes how to avoid duplicates in the future.
##1727294 - AS Java/ABAP System move functionality

For already existing system duplicates, refer to SAP Note 1694004.

Best regards,
Leo
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: how can i add a new bank in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi,

Good day!

Have you filled the Bank Country/Region as well when you created the new bank?

Normally, Bank data is associated with Bank Country/Region, which means, the new Bank should be searched after select the associated Bank Country/Region.

Best Regards, 

Ashley

entry.title: Re: Fetching deleted records in Integration center
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for reaching out. This is Hannah from Employee Central Team.

It is NOT possible to fetch deleted records in Integration center.

It is possible to fetch deleted records via "Change audit reports" tool or Table report(domain = Person and Employment Audit). 

 

Best regards,

Hannah

entry.title: Re: Free SAP Certification and practice systems for students & lecturers
entry.content:
Dear @David_Chaviano,

I am the person who contacted you last week regarding the issue of not receiving the activation email for my SAP universal account. It has been about a week since then, but I still have not received the activation email, so I am a bit concerned.

I am very eager to start learning the valuable content offered by this program. Could you please advise if there is any update or should I continue to wait a little longer?

Thank you very much for your support and assistance. I look forward to your response.

Best regards,
Dat

entry.title: Re: Timestamp in SRT_MONI Reflecting When Message Was First Sent Outbound
entry.content: I changed the tags accordingly.

entry.title: Re: Send Different Activation Emails to Newly Created Users Based on Their Account Attributes
entry.content: The issue with this post is that it doesnt explain how division is being sent. Right now the issue i have is that the division is not getting passed from SAP SuccessFactors to IAS

entry.title: クラスタ構成かつDBサーバにSAP製品がインストールされていない環境へのDVM設定について
entry.content:
お世話になっております。
弊社では、現在DVMを設定している最中です。

 

弊社の管理対象システムのいくつかは、以下の特徴を持っております。
・クラスタ構成(APサーバ2～5台、CIサーバ2台、DBサーバ2台)
・DBサーバにはHostAgentや診断エージェントがインストールされていない

 

上記の状態でも、DVMの設定は可能でしょうか？
それとも、何かしらの設定や、ツールのインストールが必要でしょうか。

English translation:
For managed systems installed distributedly(2~5 AP instances, 2 CI, 2 DB instances) where no host agent/Diagnostics agent installed for DB instances,
is DVM setting possible.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: クラスタ構成かつDBサーバにSAP製品がインストールされていない環境へのDVM設定について
entry.content:
DBサーバにHostAgentや診断エージェントがインストールされていない場合、DB情報が正常にSAP Solution Managerに認識されないため、DVM設定がエラーになる可能性が非常に高いです。
DVM設定するには、お手数ですが、まずDBサーバにHostAgentや診断エージェントをインストールお願いします。

entry.title: Re: クラスタ構成かつDBサーバにSAP製品がインストールされていない環境へのDVM設定について
entry.content: English Translation: If Host Agent/DAA agent not installed, Database instance will not be registered to LMDB correctly. So to configure DVM, please first install Host Agnet/DAA agent on DB instances.

entry.title: How to deal with the tasks listed in the Business Task Management view.
entry.content:
There are some tasks listed in the Business Task Management view, you want to know how to deal with these tasks.

  1. Go to the Application and User Management work center.
  2. Go to the Business Task Management view.
  3. Search All Items, and status=New, there are a number of tasks existing in the system.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to deal with the tasks listed in the Business Tas...
entry.content:
System creates BTM tasks when it requires input or attention from a system user. As a system administration, you can assign the task to the right person if needed. The responsible of the task need to check and take necessary actin. The tasks can be completed either automatically based on defined business logic or manually by the users.

You may get help from the help center document: Business Task Management

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/ac93025e9ac744579c43a34333b332c7.html?q=business%20task%20management&locale=en-US

Thanks,

Adie

entry.title: Re: SAP BUILD importing .mtar file error
entry.content: Hello agpekka, No, I was the only one who exported the file and tried importing. I also tried searching the lobby if there's an existing app with the same name. I also tried creating a new APP with the same name and I was able to.

entry.title: Impact on the depreciation of 3AF
entry.content:
Dear expert,

We have noticed the Scope Item 3AF has already depreciated after DIGITS. Would be any impact on the setting for Group Reporting function if we don't maintain the Group Account for each G/L of YCOA.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Impact on the depreciation of 3AF
entry.content:
Could you please check KBA 3515248 FAQ about scope item 3AF and Group Chart of account

Group reporting have nothing related with Group Account for each G/L of YCOA.

entry.title: Re: New AWS option for SAP ODP data extraction through AWS Glue
entry.content:
Dear @MarioDeFelipe 

I think there was a communication gap its because I just given at high level and typed above message in mobile so I was in hurry to give only high level picture diagram only not covering end to end including datasphere + databricks, etc.

I just given above previous picture to give representation keeping AI use cases into picture to show that post working on dataset using databricks also they can publish create or update any existing data set as well back to datasphere. 

  • I aligned with your comment and picture. Also am aware that in BDC databricks is optional. 
  • If a customer is already having databricks they can reuse same but shifting the license type from previous agreemnt to SAP databricks. (its up to customer to customer - am just saying it as a choice of switch. This info was given by SAP)
  • When coming to 300+ open connectors - am not saying every integration should go through SAP IS open connectors. Am saying when I see the datasphere demo - it has a catalog of 300+ connectors (same like IS) but this open connectors are embedded and provided inside datapshere catalog which the pipeline can use those connectors to connect to either SAP or Non-SAP or SaaS applications and get data into datapshere. Am referring to those connector catalog.
  • There are certain use cases where still Integration Suite might be required for complex integration APis or nested calls, scripting, etc. for such use cases they can leverage Integration Suite standard orchestration features and plug to Datasphere connection.
  • Also there are other customers who effectively uses API Management in Integration Suite efficiently as a central API gateway. Such customers publish all API products and APIs of SaaS applications centrally and manage at 1 place. For such strategy - BDC can still connect to IS - API Gateway and get data SaaS applications.

entry.title: Re: How should suppliers contact you then?
entry.content:
Hi! I think there are 2 options you can try with.

1.Purchase Order Output Control Settings

In business fine tuning activities, 'Purchase Order Output Control Settings', you can maintain detailed settings for Email channel. If you put buyer responsible in the CC, then when your supplier receive the mail they can also see the buyer responsible mail address in the CC and contact if needed. Once the setting is done, all the new POs sent by emails will apply the rule.


2.Supplier Master Data

In supplier master data, Communication-Collaboration setting, for each of the documents (including purchase order), you can set out output channels and the receivers. Here for email channel, you can define recipient type 'To', to supplier contacts, and 'Cc', to any contacts you want to add. This option can help if you always want a specific people to receive the mail for the specific supplier. Once the setting is done, all the new POs for the particular supplier will apply the rule.

entry.title: Re: EPPM專案計畫成本計畫收入維護
entry.content: 這個問題我已經自行解決了，謝謝。

entry.title: Re: Down payment Request Error
entry.content:
"remove the EC identifier in the tax code definition" --> where is this EC identifier, which field please.

 

"NO VAT registration numbers" --> is this on the customer master data VAT registration number that needs to be removed?

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud and what it means for SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Thanks for sharing the information. Very insightful.

entry.title: Re: Tax rate not maintain in FTXP for tax code but tax wi...
entry.content:
Hello @Priya2310 

Check if the condition line is active in the FTXP or A003 related condition is active in the FK12/12.

Regards, Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: reference a custom field in business rule
entry.content: Hello, Since the custom01 is an EP field, it is not possible to fetch it from business rule. Hope the answer helps. Best regards, Ashley

entry.title: Automatic Assignment of quantity contract to sales order line item
entry.content:
Hi All

We have a requirement where we have a quantity contract created based on departments within a ship to customer. Since the Ship to location is the same, we have mapped the individual departments to be as unloading points within a Ship to Customer. 

We have a requirement to be able to maintain a single quantity contract with the line item unloading point specifying the department within the Ship to customer. 

I would appreciate inputs from esteemed members on the following:

a. When we create a sales order, the need is to have the system determine the appropriate contract based on the unloading point entered in the line item automatically. We tried using BADI_SD_REF_DOC_CUST but it seemingly only controls the content in the pop-up window but does not assign the underlying contract to the line item.

b. When the order is received via EDI, our need is to assign the appropriate contract automatically to the sales order line item

c. If the unloading point is modified, can the reference be modified once it is assigned ?

d. A contract can be assigned at the header or can also be assigned at the line item level. What is the difference between the two options ?

Appreciate inputs from members.

entry.title: Offline Capabilities and Best Approach for Mobile App Development (BTP SDK vs MDK)?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We are developing a mobile application to simplify work order management for field maintenance teams. Due to network limitations in the areas where tasks are performed, the application needs to work offline. This means that time and material reporting must be sent to S/4HANA regardless of connectivity. As a result, whenever a technician updates information in the application, the data must be stored locally and later synchronized using the standard APIs from S/4HANA Asset Management.

After doing some research, we understand that there are two possible approaches:

  1. Build the application using Android Studio with the SAP BTP SDK for Android and SAP Mobile Services Cockpit.

  2. Build the application using SAP Mobile Development Kit (MDK) and SAP Mobile Services Cockpit.

As far as we can tell, both options support offline capabilities. However, we would like to know which option would be more suitable for our specific use case. We are particularly unsure whether both approaches fully support offline consumption of OData APIs from S/4HANA.

Thank you in advance for your guidance.

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to use Web Service as a source for Web Intelligence document?
entry.content: Yes, this works. We have an existing BOBJ WebI document and we got the URL from there and used it as an ODATA source. But i was thinking if it was possible from other ODATA source, not from BOBJ WebI document.

entry.title: Can the job requisition approver catch the dynamic role from EC?
entry.content: Can the job requisition approver catch the dynamic role from EC?

entry.title: Re: Deep entity search in Get Record Collection in SAP BUILD Apps
entry.content: Hello I am currently working on the sales offer requests feature in SAP Build Apps. For both GET and POST operations, I followed  https://discoveringabap.com/2022/11/10/building-odata-services-12-handle-deep-structures-using-expand/ and https://discoveringabap.com/2022/11/10/building-odata-services-13-create-data-using-deep-entity/ . I’ve successfully set up data retrieval (GET), and I understand the structure required for data creation (POST). However, I need a clear and detailed guide on how to implement the creation (add) functionality for a new offer request in SAP Build Apps using my custom OData service . Specifically, I would like to know how to: Properly structure the data within SAP Build Apps to perform a deep insert (parent with related child entities), Dynamically bind multiple ToItem lines, Correctly trigger the Create Record action with the full payload. Thank you in advance for your support!

entry.title: Re: XREF1, XREF2, XREF3 in MIRO - the ultimate solution
entry.content:
Hi DominikTylczyn,

May I know if this method is usable to add XREF1_HD and XREF2_HD?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Actions in Behavior Definition For CDS Custom Entity
entry.content: did you implement the read method in the handler class?

entry.title: Re: Wrong Currency Exchange Rate fetching while post Expo...
entry.content:
The Exchange Rate for FI * regards the conversion of Local Currency and Document Currency for Financial (FI) .

(A). If there is a manual entry in the Sales order field Exch.Rate Acct.  (Goto -> Header -> Accounting), then it is copied intoExchng. Rate Accntg  of Billing Document.

(B). Otherwise Exchng Rate is  determined * invoice creation from the currency tables according to the billing date. It is supposed that manual entry has higher priority.

The exchange rate for Accounting  is always determined with exchange rate type M, even though a different exchange rate type is maintained in the customer master record in this case.

Hence to conclude, in Public Cloud you can either make an Manual Entry in SO header (accounting tab > Exchange Rate Accounting) to have the same value pass onto Billing Document for that SO  or else the system will always determine it based on billing date as Exchange Rate type M.

 

__PRESENT

entry.title: Re: Send prompt from SAP GUI to AI Core service
entry.content:
You can try ABAP AI ADK with ISLM App (S/4HANA) , refer the sap help guide here 

Here is a youtube Video demo from ISLM community, wherein gen AI sdk in abap is shown along with maintenance of System and user prompts in ISLM app (with placeholders passed dynamically as the abap code executes). AI core connection is maintained for each ISLM scenario using SPRO IMG activity , wherein AI Core API ( client ID /secret) can be maintained.

entry.title: Re: Foundation Object behavior in Story Report after 1H_2...
entry.content:
Hi @lisaatkins ,

In preview release 1H 2505, some changes (FO Translation support in EC SAC Reports) are made to FO objects (defined under Manage Organisation, Pay and Job Structures) resulting in issues with Story report

We reported this to Engineering team and they suggested below workaround, and they are working on permanent fix

Step 1 :Disable FO Transalation feature: "Enable Translation of Employee Central Foundation Objects" from Provisioning UI.

Step 2 : Run "Save Changes To Report Configuration Object Job" from provisioning.

Note that both the actions are performed in provisioning, if you are the implementation partner and have access to it, you can perform above steps from your end.

Regards,

Subbu

entry.title: Re: Automated Workflow Creation AP Invoice from GRPO
entry.content: From this code, is it an example of creating a PO? Could you please share an example of how to create an A/P Invoice?

entry.title: Re: Single PR for multiple material for one vendor
entry.content:
Hi @Raymond

So basically, customer want that they only create one PR after the MRP run for all the materials that were being procured from the one vendor and don't want to create multiple PR after the MRP Run. That's why we are not thinking about creating multiple PR and club in one.

 

Pardeep

entry.title: Re: UI5 Set/Get a Global model
entry.content:
Now that sap.ui.getCore().getModel is deprecated, how do we achieve this when we want to set a model from a library, not an application?

We have a usecase where we have a utility method in a library that gets called by consuming apps. This utility is supposed to set a model that can be commonly used by all apps consuming this library. There is no Component.js or manifest here to call `this.getOwnerComponent.getModel(modelName)`.

Any suggestions?

entry.title: Unable to launch or play the existing course book or book pages on Manager.
entry.content: We developed a few courses on SEN, they were working fine, and every team member were able to access them. One fine day, no course was accessible, neither on Manager nor Producer. In both places, different error screens were showing (refer to the screenshots below).We also tried to export these course file from Manager and import them on Producer just to understand to what extent the issue is; and it turned out that courses were not getting imported on Producer.

entry.title: Re: Using SAP approuter with server-sent events
entry.content: Cache: no-transform header did not work for me. However, when i disabled compression completely, it worked!

entry.title: Re: Using SAP approuter with server-sent events
entry.content: Cache: no-transform header did not work for me. However, when i disabled compression completely, it worked!

entry.title: Re: Report to FICO to show the payroll posting details fr...
entry.content:

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-members/how-to-build-s-4-hana-model-on-payroll-posting-documents/ba-p/13905557

When I click the SetData(For Action) button, the setExchange method is called first, followed by the Read method.

Kind regards, aatan.

You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough or if you have further questions. Thank you for bringing this question to SAP Community! Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: Implicit commit
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
Can someone explain how does the Implicit Database Commit works.

There is no any dialogs steps or other items, which are simmillar with documentation, why data are comes into db?

REPORT  ZVA_EX31.
data : ls_mara type mara.
ls_mara-matnr = '000'.
insert INTO mara VALUES ls_mara.

entry.title: Re: SAP Solution Manaer 7.2 SP15 - Retrofit configuration
entry.content: Hi, You could refer to below document for general configurations of retrofit on solution manager ChaRM: https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/sm/3518046800.html For detail configuration of retrofit between ECC and S4, you may need to involve SAP consulting service, see SAP note: 2652106 for more clarification. BR.

entry.title: Re: Plateau Report Designer-Setup and Troubleshooting Tips
entry.content:
Hi @ThangND103 ,

Yes , please add the parameter in your query , accordingly it will be added in the report parameter.

let me know if facing issue with that.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Public Cloud中GUI畫面的【調整使用者介面】問題
entry.content:
Hi,

1) You can refer the document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/80d510db496a4a41b316dfb4fb5881e4/84150519d95d42fb841795f9adb249d6.html, which explains in detail, how to manage the changes made via screen personas, assign it to users and transport them.

2) If you want to make a custom field, a part of the API, you can do so via the APIs/SOAP APIs tab in the detailed view of the corresponding custom field in the manage custom field app. You will be able to view the supported APIs there.

Thanks,
Prasanth

entry.title: Re: How to remove the managed system from ChaRM - SAP Sol...
entry.content:
Hi,

For decommissioned systems, you need to go to LMDB to mark lifecycle status as 'Inactive' for them and go to ChaRM related cycles to redefine landscape to reflect the changes, also you need to go to SLAN to remove the LCGs including those decommissioned systems. 

Hope these help.

BR.

entry.title: Re: 在进行物料成本估算时，是否能只按天或者日期进行选择物料，只估算没有进行成本估算的物料，如何处理？
entry.content:
Dear,

Use Fiori app Display Materials To Be Costed, save the uncosted materials into a selection list

in costing run selection, choose the selection list

there is also dynamic selection features can be used to add more filter conditions to choose materials for costing runs.

entry.title: Re: Offline Capabilities and Best Approach for Mobile App...
entry.content: The standard SAP mobile application SAP Service and Asset Manager uses MDK in client and MAF in the server.

entry.title: Re: Options from SAP to archive HCM employee historical r...
entry.content:
Since historical data is not supposed to be updated a simple background information portlet is sufficient for your need.

you can decide the number of fields you need based on your requirements.

if these data need to be queried by api or integration make sure it’s easy enough or else you may have to switch to MDF objects.

entry.title: Re: No price could be determined for subcontracting
entry.content:
Hello,

It's mentioned in the KBA

Remove the External Processing selection from the Routing if no longer applicable.

Have you tried the same?

If the same still can't solve the issue, suggest you create customer case.

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: How should suppliers contact you then?
entry.content:
Dear Ankit,

you can define a sender email address (After the domain is DKIM verified)  via

Application and User Management

Output channel selection

 

Here you can decide from which sender domain under conditions or without will be taken as sender email.

In case of questions let me know.

Best regards

 

Didi

entry.title: Re: Slow GOS export via CMIS interface (BTP / Document Management)

I have tried by keeping the columns blank, but while import the integration fails saying that The attribute name already exists in the request payload for the <language> locale.

Please help me figure out a way.

Yugandhara

entry.title: Re: Cloud Management Tools - Major Upgrade - Announcement - 22 February 2025

entry.title: Re: Checklist Before HANA Upgrade (Brownfield)
entry.content:
Hi Nedim,

I agree with Thomas, and just adding the overview note to deploy Readiness Check and enable various functions. 
2913617 - SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA

For the customer code analyze, please choose from two options:
2185390 Enable Custom Code Analysis via Custom Code Analyzer (SYCM) BC-DWB-CEX
2781766 Enable Custom Code Analysis via ABAP Test Cockpit BC-DWB-CEX

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: ¿Que es Clean Core?
entry.content:
Recientemente algun cliente me pregunto ¿Que es Clean Core? y ¿Porque siempre dices que ni Rise o Grow with SAP se pueden hacer sin Clean Core? , y me dijo, pero con manzanitas que no entiendo si me hablas muy tecnico

Asi que me dispuse a hacerlo...

Las empresas normalmente piensen que son diferentes de los demás y que son muy complicadas o únicas.

Pero, aunque no les guste mucho, es un hecho de que el 75% a 80% de las empresas operan en su core de la misma manera.

SAP ha hecho implementaciones a lo largo del mundo por +50 años, digitalizando y automatizando los procesos, creo que esta base de conocimiento sustenta su aseveración. Pero si a ti, como a mi te gustan los datos, para sustentarlo te cuento… El 77 % de los ingresos por transacciones a nivel global interactúan con un sistema SAP.  Los clientes usuarios SAP globales se incluyen *el 92 % de las empresas de la lista Forbes Global 2000, *el 98 % de las 100 marcas más valiosas y * el 100 % de las empresas con las mejores calificaciones de sostenibilidad del índice Dow Jones.

Muchos de los procesos o de las capacidades de negocio de estas empresas independientemente de su industria, área geográfica o tamaño son exactamente iguales – Puedes preguntar a algún arquitecto empresarial si dudas de mi aseveración 😉

Todas tienen que pagar taxes, tienen que hacer sus cuentas por pagar, cuentas por cobrar, administrar sus activos, pagan nomina o proveedores, cobran a sus clientes etc…

Y Aunque todas empresas quieren hacer todo esto realmente muy bien, realmente los clientes finales no compran a estas empresas por eso, sino por lo que las diferencia de las otras.

Y exactamente de eso se trata Clean Core 

Identificar las capacidades que NO son diferenciadoras, sacarlas a luz, reconocerlas, tenerlas claras.

No se trata de aventarlas al cajon feo. Hay que digitalizarlas con Best Practices, optimizarlas con modelos preconfigurados o creando procesos en Build, incluso para cargas importantes de tareas, hay que infusionarlas automatizando lo que sea posible usando AI o RPA, para que sigan mejorando.

Pero son ESTÁNDAR, nuestro foco principal no es ese.

¡Como empresa luego nos diferenciaremos fuera de ese clean core!

Esto de mantener el nucleo limpio había sido el sueño de los Configuradores, Desarrolladores, Project Managers o Directivos, tanto de SAP como de nosotros los partners como nosotros e incluso de varios de los empleados de IT de las empresas Usuarias. Si a ti, no te ha tocado personalmente, pregunta a alguien que haya querido hacer una integración o implementar un módulo o una solución o realizar upgrade de enhacement package o de versión o incluso simplemente instalar una nota para resolver una situación o habilitar una localización… Esto durante muchos años a implicado una experiencia religiosa que involucra mucha inversión de dinero, de tiempo de usuarios clave, de consultores, desarrolladores, además de usuarios claves y del equipo completo interno de IT del cliente. Logrando no siempre el objetivo en el tiempo comprometido. Mientras algunos vendedores de SAP seguían diciendo a los clientes que todo era sencillo y rapidisimo, como plug a play 🔌…. Mientras nosotros así 😬 

En mi opinión, habíamos tardado en poner tanto énfasis en esto.

Pero ¿por qué ahora si están haciendo caso la mayoría de las empresas del mundo a nuestro llamado?

Creo que el contexto de ahora influencia: durante muchos años hablamos de la disrupción, pero hasta que nos tocaron todas juntas (pandemias, guerras, cambios climáticos, hiper inflación, crisis energéticas) las empresas realmente interiorizaron que tenían que ser más flexibles y agiles y que esto no era optativo, sino que se trata de cuestión de sobrevivencia.

Y la manera de tener agilidad y flexibilidad no es con un set complejo de procesos y capacidades, en sistemas que son complicados, caros de mantener y por ende frágiles: ¡Se logra manteniendo el núcleo limpio, con CLEAN CORE!

¿Cómo saber que es realmente diferenciador? 

Metodológicamente, con un ejercicio de arquitectura empresarial que documenta, analiza y clasifica las capacidades de negocio y que crea en conjunto con los C-Levels y Owners un mapa de calor donde estas capacidades son muy claras.

¿Cómo lograr alinear a la empresa para que sea realmente diferenciador? 

Implementando una Estructura de Gobernanza para Clean Core, las políticas corporativas se construyen alrededor de él. SIEMPRE manteniendo la codificación limpia, los datos LEAN, las modificaciones controladas, el ambiente confiable, los procesos claros y las operaciones efectivas y eficientes

Por ejemplo: Si uno de nuestros Goals de la estructura de Gobernanza es tener Reportes Financieros Estándares para lograr una consolidación ágil,

y el Chief de Finanzas de algún país habla con el Chief de Finanzas Global o con el CEO, y solicita una excepción alegando que la operación financiera es muy complicadas o única (si exactamente como decía al comienzo ).El Clean Core Commitee (llamado SAP Standarization Board) formado por el Cliente y el Partner, evaluara la solicitud, y rechazara la inclusión del desarrollo en el Backlog.

La respuesta será simple y firme, pues la conciencia de costo beneficio sigue vigente y, además, la política corporativa es clara. Será entre imposible y muy difícil saltarla, incluso a ese nivel.

Entonces se mantiene el beneficio de procesar la consolidación financiera en cuestión de horas y no de días, además de haber ahorrado una cantidad importante de diner.  Pero, además, podremos conectar nuevos módulos o nuevas soluciones SAP, hacer cambios de versión y beneficiarnos de las innovaciones sin tener que pasar por un calvario técnico. 

Así pues, lo decisivo para aprovechar esto -Si las empresas deciden adoptar el Clean Core-, actuar en consecuencia siempre, No matter what or who.

La gritojerarquia debe ser eliminada. Las decisiones se toman en base al bien común de la empresa, no de los departamentos y/ de los egos de sus managers.

SI realmente no están tan seguros a todos los niveles de querer apostar por esto al 100%, mejor es recomendar a los clientes que lo dejen para después.

Clean Core es Gobernanza, una Mentalidad, Una Metodología, un Cambio Organizacional, Liderazgo de Cambio, y por último es TECNOLOGÍA.

Clean Core ayuda para que después de limpiar haya orden, cero permisividad, e impedir mezclar las cosas se vuelvan complicadas.

Me encanta como lo explico una vez, Paul Saunders de SAP “Vamos a tener una mentalidad tipo Marie Kondo: disfrutando todo limpio, defendiendo solo lo que suma, decidiendo sabiamente y manteniendo sistema de organización eficaz”.

Tecnológicamente Clean Core significa que si no necesitamos hacer algo no lo hagamos. Y si necesitamos hacer algo hagámoslo tan simple como sea posible, tan eficiente como sea posible, tan minimalista como sea posible. Usemos de soluciones como están, en lugar de sobremodificar las soluciones y terminar con Frankensteins  poco eficientes, caros de mantener y poco sostenibles en el tiempo.Si se trata de limpiar, el momento mejor para esto es durante tu transformación tecnológica mientras viajas de ECC a S/4Hana con Rise o Grow.

Lograr que tu conversión sea Limpia no será fácil, pero rendirá sus frutos durante la misma conversión.

Si tanto nosotros como Partner y los Clientes realmente lo abrazan y lo llevan a cabo adecuadamente, (y no simplemente barremos las cosas debajo de la alfombra, solo para ocultarlas),  se podría tratar del Proyecto de sus Sueños. Nunca, ¡se los firmo!, nunca habrán vivido un proyecto tan smooth. 

Pero lo más importante, y lo divertido viene incluso después. Toca atender lo que NO es CORE, lo que llamamos diferenciación.  Aqui podremos hacer las cosas que realmente nos atraen, las cosas que realmente impulsan la visión y la misión de las empresas. 

EL juego de los adultos…  INNOVAR!. 

Ahí es donde nuestros super poderes de El talento, la creatividad, la innovación está en el 20% que NO es Core, en lo que llamamos diferenciación. Clean Core implica poner todos los esfuerzos directamente a nuestras capacidades diferenciadoras. Eso que realmente hace que los clientes, le compren a nuestras Empresas Clientes. Eso lo que estos clientes encuentran que les agrega valor, y esto es que la empresa pueda adaptarse a los cambios en el mercado, en mi consumo, en el mundo, es decir a las demandas del cliente.

Estas "adaptaciones" siendo Clean Core pueden ser 

* La extensibilidad en la Aplicación que permite a los configuradores funcionales personalizar las aplicaciones de SAP Fiori y desarrollar varios componentes con low code o no code con SAP Build.

Tambien puede implicar consumir soluciones del SAP Business Accelerator Hub que cuenta conAPI empaquetadas, servicios comerciales y aplicaciones disponibles, listos para customizarse. 

* La extensibilidad de desarrollador permite a los desarrolladores de ABAP ampliar procesos, crear servicios OData y crear API mientras aíslan el código personalizado.

* y La extensibilidad en paralelo, aquí es donde SAP Business Technology Platform y SAP Integration Suite (antes CPI) se hacen presentes, reuniendo una colección de aplicaciones y procesos (ya desarrolladas, ready to use, integrados y flexibles), así como un framework con su propia metodología, lenguaje, ambientes que sirven para crear inteligencia artificial, para desarrollar de aplicaciones, y costumizar Integraciones, en un entorno unificado.

BTP es el conductor, el orquestador, el integrador, entre estas piezas y el Core.

BTP es quien nos permite tomar las partes de funcionalidad adecuadas para cada capacidad de negocio, de los diferentes sistemas en la solución, con el objetivo de proveer un resultado efectivo para el cliente final.

entry.title: Re: FRUN HARDWARE MIGRATION
entry.content:
Hi Calvin,

System copy normally results in the hostname change. Changing the hostname, changes the FRUN GUID in the LMDB and it leads the FRUN and managed system configuration missing. This is what the FRUN master note mentioned that System Copy is not supported yet. 

Keeping the same VM hostname, SID, installation number can maximum preserve the existing configurations. Here are some important hints that SAP Focused Run system related:

1. Keep the SAP Web Dispatcher URL stable to make sure SDA to FRUN communication works smoothly.

2. Make sure that in LMDB the old Focused Run System is updated after system move with the new SLD data supplier. 

3. Moving the FRUN to a new DB server changes the LMDB GUID, which more or less affects the applications. This is a case-by-case discussion with all applications involved. So, it is easier to ensure FRUN GUID is stable that's to make sure SAPDBHOST, SID, Installation unchanged (which is in fact relatively easy).

4. Make sure the ordering and you have a fallback option in case of issues.

Best regards,
Vera Jiang
